dialogueChunks.plotStep: It took hours of talking, but they agreed to return. I apologized in front of the whole settlement: told everyone the truth about {quest_focus} and what I'd done. {target_npc} looked me in the eye and said, 'We've been neighbors too long for one mistake to end it.' I didn't deserve that grace. {quest_threat} was nothing but my own paranoia.
dialogueChunks.outro: You carried the truth when I was too blinded by suspicion to see it. This is yours, with my deepest gratitude.

playerResponses.text: Grace given freely is the rarest thing in the world. Treasure it.
Next time, talk to someone before you accuse them.
I'm glad it worked out. Take better care of your friendships.

dialogueChunks.intro: The debt is paid, {player_name}. The trader took the {quest_item} and left without trouble.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} broke down when I told them what you'd done. Not anger: relief. {quest_stakes} was mended when the settlement rallied around them once they heard the truth. Three families offered to help if collectors come back. We're closer than before, believe it or not.
dialogueChunks.outro: My jealousy nearly destroyed someone, but your persistence turned it into something better. Take this: I set it aside from my own savings.

playerResponses.text: Sometimes the worst mistakes lead to the best outcomes. Hold onto this.
Just remember this feeling next time suspicion starts whispering.

dialogueChunks.intro: It's over, {player_name}. The truth is out, and I feel lighter and heavier at the same time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc}'s relative is recovering, thanks to the {quest_item} you brought. And {target_npc} has forgiven me, though they said it'll take time. I understand that. The settlement is quieter now: people being more careful with their assumptions. {quest_threat} turned out to be nothing but my own imagination.
dialogueChunks.outro: You saw through my suspicion to the truth underneath. {quest_stakes} can heal. Please, take this with my thanks.

playerResponses.text: Trust takes time to rebuild. But the fact that you're trying says a lot.
Lessons learned the hard way stick the longest. Be well.
